post.content:
Status: 6 YOE
Location: Seattle

Phone screen (1 hour):

Asked to design an API limiter such that "makeHttpRequest" can only be called 4 times a second. Any more requests within the second are rejected.

Was there more to this problem? Like what about a client id, and rate limiting per client? If the question is just this, then maybe we can use a queue of size 4 and put unix timestamps into it. Front will be oldest and rear will be latest call timestamps. Every time a request comes in, remove from front till you\'re in 1 second window, and check if the new request can fit into the queue.
